title: Release mechanism for trapped air in a fluid flow device
abstract: Mechanisms and methods for minimizing trapped air in a fluid flow device are disclosed. An example device includes a first chamber having an inlet and outlet for substantially horizontal fluid flow, a second chamber at least partially openly disposed above the first chamber, and a tube vertically disposed in the second chamber from the second chamber ceiling to the first chamber. When fluid flows from the inlet to the outlet, at least a portion of the fluid flows into the second chamber and air collected at its ceiling moves down the tube and into the first chamber. The ceiling of the second chamber may be contoured such that a first internal height at a first location of the second chamber is greater than a second internal height at a second location, forming a pocket at the ceiling at the first location where air can collect before entering the tube.
claims:
Claims What Is Claimed Is: 1. A fluid flow device configured to minimize trapped air within, comprising: a first chamber having an inlet for incoming fluid and an outlet for outgoing fluid to allow for substantially horizontal flow of fluid; and a second chamber at least partially openly disposed above the first chamber, the second chamber having a ceiling contoured such that a first internal height is located at a first location of the second chamber and a second internal height is located at a second location of the second chamber, wherein the first internal height is greater than the second internal height, thereby forming a pocket at the ceiling at the first location, wherein when fluid flows from the inlet to the outlet of the first chamber, at least a portion of the fluid flows into the second chamber and air moves into the pocket and away from the second location of the second chamber ceiling. 2. The fluid flow device of claim 1, wherein the second chamber ceiling includes a channel indentation to direct air across the ceiling from the second location to the first location. 3. The fluid flow device of claim 1 or 2, further comprising: a tube vertically disposed in the second chamber, the tube having a first open end in close proximity of the second chamber ceiling near the first location and a second open end opening into the first chamber, wherein when fluid flows from the inlet to the outlet of the first chamber, at least a portion of the fluid flows into the second chamber and air collected at the ceiling of the second chamber moves down the tube and into the first chamber to minimize an amount of air trapped in the second chamber. 4. The fluid flow device of claim 3, wherein the second chamber ceiling includes a channel indentation to direct air across the ceiling from the second location to the first location. 5. The fluid flow device of any of claims 1 to 4, wherein the first location is at an outer edge of the second chamber. 6. The fluid flow device of any of claims 1 to 4, wherein the first location is at a center of the second chamber. 7. A fluid meter configured to minimize trapped air within, comprising: a reservoir can having an inlet for incoming fluid and an outlet for outgoing fluid that allows for substantially horizontal fluid flow, and having a reservoir between the inlet and the outlet; a stator component having a top portion disposed on top of the reservoir can and a bottom portion that extends into the reservoir for supporting a measurement device; and a gear box above the stator component housing a coupling between the measurement device and a register, the coupling configured to transmit measurement information from the measurement device to the register to measure fluid flow, the gear box at least partially openly disposed above the reservoir, wherein a ceiling of the gear box is contoured such that a first internal height is located at a first location of the gear box and a second internal height is located at a second location of the gear box, wherein the first internal height is greater than the second internal height, to allow air to collect in a pocket formed at the ceiling at the first location and away from the second location when fluid flows through the reservoir and the gear box. 8. The fluid meter of claim 7, wherein the gear box ceiling includes a channel indentation to direct air across the ceiling from the second location to the first location. 9. The fluid meter of claim 7 or 8, further comprising: a tube that extends from the top portion of the stator component toward the ceiling of the gear box, the tube disposed near the first location, the tube having a top that is open, a bottom that is open to the reservoir, and a height that is greater than the second internal height of the gear box and less than the first internal height of the gear box, to allow air to flow from the gear box into the reservoir when fluid flows through the reservoir and the gear box such that an amount of air trapped in the gear box is minimized. 10. The fluid meter of claim 9, wherein the gear box ceiling includes a channel indentation to direct air across the ceiling from the second location to the first location. 11. The fluid meter of claim 9 or 10, wherein the tube is molded as part of the top portion of the stator component. 12. The fluid meter of claim 9 or 10, wherein the tube is a standalone part fitted through a hole in the top portion of the stator component. 13. The fluid meter of any of claims 7 to 12, wherein the first location is at an outer edge of the gear box. 14. The fluid meter of any of claims 7 to 12, wherein the first location is at a center of the gear box. 15. A fluid meter configured to minimize trapped air within, comprising: a reservoir can having an inlet for incoming fluid and an outlet for outgoing fluid that allows for substantially horizontal fluid flow, and having a reservoir between the inlet and the outlet; a stator component having a top portion disposed on top of the reservoir can and a bottom portion that extends into the reservoir for supporting a measurement device; a gear box above the stator component housing a coupling between the measurement device and a register, the coupling configured to transmit measurement information from the measurement device to the register to measure fluid flow, the gear box at least partially openly disposed above the reservoir; and a tube that extends from the top portion of the stator component toward a ceiling of the gear box, wherein a top of the tube is open and in close proximity to the gear box ceiling, and wherein a bottom of the tube is open to the reservoir, to allow air to flow from the gear box into the reservoir when fluid flows through the reservoir and the gear box to minimize an amount of air trapped in the gear box, wherein the gear box ceiling is contoured such that a first internal height is located at a first location of the gear box and a second internal height is located at a second location of the gear box, wherein the first internal height is greater than the second internal height, wherein the tube is disposed near the first location, and wherein a height of the tube is greater than the second internal height and less than the first internal height. 16. The fluid meter of claim 15, wherein the ceiling of the gear box includes a channel indentation to direct air across the ceiling from the second location to the first location. 17. The fluid meter of claim 15 or 16, wherein the tube is molded as part of the top portion of the stator component. 18. The fluid meter of claim 15 or 16, wherein the tube is a standalone part fitted through a hole in the top portion of the stator component. 19. The fluid meter of any of claims 15 to 18, wherein the first location is at an outer edge of the gear box. 20. The fluid meter of any of claims 15 to 18, wherein the first location is at a center of the gear box. 21. A fluid flow device configured to minimize trapped air within, comprising: a first chamber having an inlet for incoming fluid and an outlet for outgoing fluid to allow for substantially horizontal flow of fluid; a second chamber at least partially openly disposed above the first chamber; and a tube vertically disposed in the second chamber, the tube having a first open end in close proximity of a ceiling of the second chamber and a second open end opening into the first chamber, wherein when fluid flows from the inlet to the outlet of the first chamber, at least a portion of the fluid flows into the second chamber and air collected at the ceiling of the second chamber moves down the tube and into the first chamber to minimize an amount of air trapped in the second chamber, and wherein the ceiling of the second chamber is contoured such that a first internal height is located at a first location of the second chamber and a second internal height is located at a second location of the second chamber, wherein the first internal height is greater than the second internal height, wherein the tube is disposed near the first location, and wherein a height of the tube is greater than the second internal height and less than the first internal height. 22. The fluid flow device of claim 21, wherein the ceiling of the second chamber includes a channel indentation to direct air across the ceiling from the second location to the first location. 23. The fluid flow device of claim 21 or 22, wherein the first location is at one of an outer edge of the second chamber or at a center of the second chamber. 24. A method of minimizing trapped air in a fluid flow device, the method comprising: directing a flow of fluid substantially horizontally into an inlet and out of an outlet of a first chamber; and directing at least a portion of the fluid from the first chamber into a second chamber, the second chamber at least partially openly disposed above the first chamber, wherein the directing the at least a portion of the fluid from the first chamber into the second chamber includes allowing air collected at the ceiling of the second chamber to flow to the first chamber through a tube disposed vertically in the second chamber, the tube having a first open end in close proximity of a ceiling of the second chamber and a second open end opening into the first chamber, wherein the ceiling of the second chamber is contoured such that a first internal height is located at a first location of the second chamber and a second internal height is located at a second location of the second chamber, wherein the first internal height is greater than the second internal height, thereby forming a pocket at the ceiling at the first location, wherein the tube is disposed near the first location, and wherein a height of the tube is greater than the second internal height and less than the first internal height of the second chamber, such that the air collected at the ceiling of the second chamber is displaced by the fluid and moves into the pocket and into the tube. 25. The method of claim 24, wherein the first location is at one of an outer edge of the second chamber or at a center of the second chamber. 26. A method of minimizing air trapped in a fluid flow device, the method comprising: directing a flow of fluid substantially horizontally into an inlet and out of an outlet of a first chamber; and 

 directing at least a portion of the fluid from the first chamber into a second chamber, the second chamber at least partially openly disposed above the first chamber and having a ceiling contoured such that a first internal height located at a first location of the second chamber is greater than a second internal height located at a second location of the second chamber, thereby forming a pocket at the ceiling at the first location, wherein the directing the at least a portion of the fluid from the first chamber into the second chamber includes allowing air collected at the ceiling of the second chamber to move into the pocket and away from the second location of the second chamber ceiling. 27. The method of claim 26, wherein the directing the at least a portion of the fluid from the first chamber into the second chamber includes allowing air collected at the ceiling of the second chamber to flow to the first chamber through a tube disposed vertically in the second chamber, the tube having a first open end in the pocket and a second open end opening into the first chamber. 28. The method of claim 26 or 27, wherein the first location is at one of an outer edge of the second chamber or at a center of the second chamber. 29. A method of minimizing trapped air in a fluid meter, comprising: providing a reservoir can having an inlet for incoming fluid and an outlet for outgoing fluid that allows for substantially horizontal fluid flow, with a reservoir between the inlet and the outlet; positioning a stator component in the reservoir, the stator component having a top portion disposed on top of the reservoir can and a bottom portion that extends into the reservoir for supporting a measurement device; positioning a gear box above the stator component, the gear box housing a coupling between the measurement device and a register, the coupling configured to transmit measurement information from the measurement device to the register to measure fluid flow, the gear box at least partially openly disposed above the reservoir; positioning a tube to extend from the top portion of the stator component toward a ceiling of the gear box, a top of the tube open and in close proximity to the gear box ceiling, and a bottom of the tube open to the reservoir, the tube positioned to allow air to flow from 

 the gear box into the reservoir when fluid flows through the reservoir and the gear box to minimize an amount of air trapped in the gear box; and contouring the gear box ceiling such that a first internal height is located at a first location of the gear box and a second internal height is located at a second location of the gear box, wherein the first internal height is greater than the second internal height, wherein the tube is positioned near the first location, and wherein a height of the tube is greater than the second internal height of the gear box and less than the first internal height of the gear box. 30. The method of claim 29, wherein the positioning of the tube includes molding the tube as part of the top portion of the stator component. 31. The method of claim 29, wherein the positioning of the tube includes fitting the tube through a hole in the top portion of the stator component. 32. The method of any of claims 29 to 31 , further comprising: providing a channel indentation in the ceiling of the gear box to direct air across the ceiling from the second location to the first location. 33. The method of any of claims 29 to 32, wherein the first location is at one of an outer edge of the gear box or at a center of the gear box. 34. A method of minimizing trapped air in a fluid meter, comprising: providing a reservoir can having an inlet for incoming fluid and an outlet for outgoing fluid that allows for substantially horizontal fluid flow, with a reservoir between the inlet and the outlet; positioning a stator component in the reservoir, the stator component having a top portion disposed on top of the reservoir can and a bottom portion that extends into the reservoir for supporting a measurement device; and positioning a gear box above the stator component, the gear box housing a coupling between the measurement device and a register, the coupling configured to transmit measurement information from the measurement device to the register to measure fluid flow, the gear box at least partially openly disposed above the reservoir, wherein a ceiling of the 

 gear box is contoured such that a first internal height is located at a first location of the gear box and a second internal height is located at a second location of the gear box, wherein the first internal height is greater than the second internal height, to allow air to collect in a pocket formed at the ceiling at the first location and away from the second location when fluid flows through the reservoir and the gear box. 35. The method of claim 34, further comprising: providing a channel indentation in the ceiling of the gear box to direct air across the ceiling from the second location to the first location. 36. The method of claim 34 or 35, further comprising: positioning a tube to extend from the top portion of the stator component toward a ceiling of the gear box, a top of the tube open and in close proximity to the gear box ceiling, and a bottom of the tube open to the reservoir, the tube positioned near the first location of the gear box, wherein a height of the tube is greater than the second internal height of the gear box and less than the first internal height of the gear box, and wherein the tube is positioned to allow air to flow from the gear box into the reservoir when fluid flows through the reservoir and the gear box to minimize an amount of trapped air in the gear box. 37. The method of claim 36, further comprising: providing a channel indentation in the ceiling of the gear box to direct air across the ceiling from the second location to the first location. 38. The method of any of claims 34 to 37, wherein the first location is at one of an outer edge of the gear box or at a center of the gear box. 39. A mechanism to minimize air in a fluid flow device, comprising: a tube vertically disposed in a first chamber, the tube having a first open end in close proximity of a ceiling of the first chamber and a second open end opening into a second chamber at least partially openly disposed below the first chamber, the second chamber having an inlet for incoming fluid and an outlet for outgoing fluid to allow for substantially horizontal flow of fluid, 

 wherein when fluid flows into the inlet of the second chamber, at least a portion of the fluid flows into the first chamber and air collected at the ceiling of the first chamber moves down the tube and into the second chamber, and wherein the ceiling of the first chamber is contoured such that a first internal height at a first location of the first chamber is greater than a second internal height at a second location of the first chamber, wherein the tube is disposed near the first location, and wherein a height of the tube is greater than the second internal height of the first chamber and less than the first internal height of the first chamber. 40. The mechanism of claim 39, wherein the ceiling of the first chamber includes a channel indentation to direct air across the ceiling from the second location to the first location. 41. The mechanism of claim 39 or 40, wherein the first location is at one of an outer edge of the first chamber or at a center of the first chamber.
